blocks.content: Flash! As ten or so people simultaneously cast teleportation spells, the surroundings briefly lit up as bright as day.

blocks.speaker: Beam
blocks.content: Rampaging Evolution!
Kraygus, attacked Gaold.
Its basic form was a mantis, standing two meters tall, with the deadly traits of eleven different poisonous insects.
Pooof!
Gas bombs exploded, followed by flames, and various poisonous stingers shot out.
With Rose's pheromones enhancing their power, the effects were multiplied.
Black, a crowd control mage, cast petrification magic on Gaold's legs.
Curse magic Influenza was cast on Gaold, and Iron Wall Rider erected barriers in all directions to restrict Gaold's movement.
Kraygus's poisonous gas filled the enclosed space, obscuring vision.
Gaold likely didn't expect anyone else to be in the toxic gas chamber with him.
The key player was the carnivore addict, Benifis.

blocks.speaker: Benifis
blocks.content: Just one bite, that's all I need.
Benifis's flesh began to rot.
Already poisoned, he wouldn't live for more than 20 minutes, but he was the only one among the team who could avoid the mage's shared senses.

blocks.speaker: Benifis
blocks.content: Hurry up and show your skills, you damn bastard.
Once a spell is cast, it becomes a phenomenon that affects even the caster.
So why was Gaold unaffected by the air press?
Because he cast his magic outside his own range, and Benifis aimed for that moment.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Ha ha, it's been a while since my blood boiled like this.
As soon as Gaold's voice was heard, an immense atmospheric pressure crushed the entire space.
Screeech!
Kraygus was flattened, and the gas settled on the ground.
In the now-clear landscape, Benifis charged at Gaold from behind.

blocks.speaker: Benifis
blocks.content: Now!

blocks.speaker: Benifis
blocks.content: Bite!
At that moment, Gaold's lips curled into a grin that reached his ears.
Guh!
Crunch!
Benifis's jaw clenched involuntarily. Before he could swallow the flesh, his neck twisted rapidly.
Crack! Thud!
His body fell away, leaving only Benifis's face on Gaold's shoulder.
Gaold grabbed the bald head, tore the face off, and threw it to the ground.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: One down.
In the landscape filled with afterimages, Gaold grinned wickedly.
Cage B Team had 20 members left.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Gaold was…
He was the gentlest and most foolish person I knew. He was inherently kind. He was raised in the Yore faith, but even without it, he would have been the same.
Religion doesn't define a person's character.
Shirone knew many who had oppressed others in the name of religion.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Gaold loved flowers.
Pfft!

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Oh, sorry.
He loved tending to flower beds. Whenever his grades in the graduation class were bad, he would go to the flower bed and plant flowers.
Kangnan's eyes grew somber.
She didn't know Gaold during his school days, but she had many memories of him.
Sein's gaze turned to the night sky.
The only thing worth acknowledging was his stubbornness, which was almost foolish. Even in the graduation class of the magic school, he never cast an offensive spell.
Gaold loses. Three points deducted. Sein wins. Three points added.
After the graduation class's highlight, the duel, Gaold gritted his teeth.
Sein found it amusing.
Honestly, Gaold wasn't particularly talented in magic. The only thing he was somewhat good at was air magic, a basic form of omnipotence.
In such a situation, only defending with non-combat magic, who could he possibly beat?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: There's no need to feel wronged. Even if you had cast offensive magic, you wouldn't have been a match for me.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I'm not upset because I lost. I'm frustrated because I couldn't prove my faith.
Sein sighed.
Gaold's best magic was air press. To be blunt, it wasn't even a technique, just a way to increase atmospheric pressure to protect himself.
If he could last ten minutes, it would be a draw.
That was the only victory Gaold sought.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: 'Foolish. The world is full of people who will do anything to win.'
Gaold wouldn't graduate.
He was the epitome of the illogical thinking Sein despised most.
Then one day.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Hello? Long time no see. You two still getting along?
Miro approached while Gaold and Sein were in the middle of a religious debate.
The bizarre incident on the first day had led the three of them to often hang out together in the graduation class.
All three were unusual individuals, but they formed a strange golden balance in their precarious relationship.
Recently, however, Miro had been frequently absent from school.
It wasn't against the rules, but not knowing what she was up to frustrated Sein.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Getting along? Me with this guy?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hello? Where have you been this time?

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Oh, I've been busy. Getting called here and there.
Miro's skin was tanned.
Since it wasn't a particularly sunny season, Sein guessed she had been outside Tormia.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Ha ha! Sounds like fun.
Gaold simply liked Miro.
As he got to know her, he realized she was far more talented than he had thought.
She was the first person in the school's history to pass the seventh level of the survival test last year.
But what he really liked about her was her open-minded thinking.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Oh, right. Miro, listen to this. Is it really such a strange logic that God knows love?

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: God doesn't know love. Love is a purely human emotion. If God exists, it would be a being with feelings and thoughts completely different from what we know.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Even if that's true, it doesn't fit your definition of God. God is a perfect being, so it's natural for God to understand human emotions. Therefore, it's only natural for God to know love.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Gaold looked at Miro.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: What do you think?
Miro didn't answer for a long time.
Gaold, who thought she would naturally side with him due to her endlessly free thinking, found the situation unexpected.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Gaold, there is no God.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: …What?

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: God doesn't exist. Everything is determined by dimensions. Within the algorithm of dimensions, hierarchies are divided.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: What are you talking about!
Gaold felt betrayed.
Even if everyone else denied God, Miro shouldn't.
Because…

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Determined by dimensions? Miro, you haven't seen all dimensions. How can you say that?
Miro smiled bitterly.
It was only later that they speculated she had met Goffin around this time.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Yeah! No one knows! Sorry. Let's stop talking about this and eat. Who's buying me lunch?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Forget it! I'm leaving.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: What, is he sulking? So sensitive.
Being kind-hearted was good, but the problem was his narrow-mindedness.
It was probably because he was soft-hearted, but Miro felt unnecessarily guilty and didn't like it.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Hey, you.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: For the first time, I like you.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Miro sighed and unfocused her eyes.
As with any school, Alpheas Magic School had its infamous survival test day.
The 30 graduation students were divided into teams, with a ratio of 29 to 1.
It wasn't surprising that they wanted to at least break even since Miro had passed the seventh level last year.
What was truly surprising was that Gaold had given up even that.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Gaold, are you really okay?
Some students asked with concern.
His skills weren't enough to be considered a rival, but his personality was good, so they showed some kindness.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hmph, I will never side with someone who changes their words like a bat.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Ho ho ho! Fine, I'll show you the spicy side of this genius girl.
Gaold gritted his teeth.
He was furious at how she could be so confident after hurting his feelings.
The survival test began, and a gruesome hell unfolded in stages.
As people were ejected from the virtual zone, they soon entered the fifth stage, 'Torture.'

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: When Sein mentioned that he had been ejected at the fifth stage to Shirone's group, he hesitated.
But Shirone thought that just the fact that the logic-focused Servant had crossed the threshold showed the potential of Equilibrium, which manipulates both emotion and reason.
And Gaold… surprisingly reached the sixth stage, 'Extreme Heat.' Even the graduation students couldn't believe it.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: God, sacrifice this lamb to save everyone. Bring love and peace to the world.
In the hellfire, Gaold knelt and prayed with his hands clasped.
The terrifying heat seeped through his teeth, and juice, not tears, flowed from his eyes, but his prayer didn't stop.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Give up, Gaold. You'll really die if you keep this up.
Even in the extreme heat, her expression remained composed.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: God, please… burn me…
Miro couldn't leave Gaold like this.
If his body was completely burned away, his spirit would leave his body as well.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Why are you doing this? This is just a magic school test. What are you trying to prove about God's existence here?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I'm not trying to prove God. I can endure because God resides within me.
Miro let out a hot breath.
How could someone be so stubborn?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: And…

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I don't want to lose to you.
Miro understood.
It wasn't just Gaold; countless mages in the world longed to surpass her.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Alright. But you don't have to do this. The survival test isn't everything. You can work harder and beat me in the graduation exam.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: God, I believe in the power of infinite love. Grant me…

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Why are you doing this!
Miro grabbed Gaold by the collar and lifted him up.
Just that action made Gaold's body jerk in reality.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: You live under constant criticism anyway! Whether God exists or not, is it worth risking your life over a single word from me? Do you want to die and make me feel guilty? If so, you're mistaken. No matter what…

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Because I like you.
Miro's words stopped.
All the students watching through the vision were stunned. Only Sein watched with a serious expression.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: What… what did you say?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I said I like you. So I want you to understand me. I want you to say I'm right.
Miro let go of his collar.
She had no words for the confession she heard in hell.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: So… you want to date me?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Gaold nodded.
His eyes burned with heat and passion, but Miro responded simply, as usual.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: But I don't like you.
Boom!
The intense heat exploded from Gaold's head, and he lost consciousness.
Thus, Gaold failed the survival test, and Sein recalled that day, saying,

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: In a way, Gaold saved face. He didn't fail because of his religious beliefs. But he did become the biggest fool. Everyone laughed at him, calling him the first man to get rejected in hell.
In the end, Gaold's pure love was brutally rejected by Miro.
But before moving on to the most important event, Sein added one more thing.
The truth is… he still doesn't fully understand it.
ΓÇö
